short_description: Easily test the karat content of gold with ToolTreaux’s 10k Gold Testing Kit with Testing Stone and Neutralizer Combo! GOLD TESTING METHOD: This gold testing method is best used on gold that is yet to be smelted, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item.
narrative: Easily test the karat content of gold with ToolTreaux’s 10K Gold Testing Kit with Testing Stone and Neutralizer Combo! The kit includes one 0.5oz bottle of gold testing solution, one 0.5oz bottle of neutralizing solution, and one testing stone. The included testing solution is potent, effective and will see you through dozens of tests! Housed in small, sturdy, shatterproof plastic squeeze bottles, the testing solution bottles are easy to maneuver, giving the tester control over the amount of solution used. GOLD TESTING METHOD: This gold testing method is best used on gold that is yet to be smelted, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item. If testing fine jewelry or other finished gold items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot, so as not to compromise the finished piece. INSTRUCTIONS: To test gold karat content, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit. Place a small drop of solution on top of the deposit; if the gold is the same karat or higher, the solution will not affect the deposit. If the deposit is of a lower karat, the deposit will dissolve and eventually disappear. When done, add an equal amount of neutralizing solution to neutralize the gold solution. Ensure gold testing stone has been thoroughly washed and neutralized before starting any more testing! PLEASE NOTE: Testing solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Ensure neutralization of the testing solution after every test by using the neutralizing solution. Read the label on the bottle and follow provided instructions.

EASY USE: Comes with guided instructions; simply place a drop of testing solution on your gold sample deposit and instantly see the results
SAFETY FIRT: Included neutralizing solution ensures safe practice and clean up; easily neutralize the harsh chemicals in the testing solution once you are done

Place a small drop solution over the top of the deposit; the purity of the silver is approximated according to the color the solution turns when it is applied to the sample. If the solution turns bright red, that indicates high quality silver. If the solution turns blue or green, the sample may contain brass or copper. If the sample deposit dissolves quickly, or the solution turns a dark brown or yellow, it is likely a base metal or silver plated. When done, add an equal amount of neutralizing solution to neutralize the silver solution. Ensure the testing stone has been thoroughly washed and neutralized before performing any testing. narrative: Whether you're an avid gold prospector or just dipping your toes into this fun outdoor hobby, gold panning with a pocket sluice box will help ensure you have the best prospecting experience. Made from high quality TPR (Thermoplastic Rubber) rubber this sluice box will continuously provide high yielding results of fine gold from your pay dirt. Known for being lightweight, flexible and UV resistant, this high-contrast green rubber material is ideal for a pocket sluice because it is easy to clean up and clear out once your gold pay dirt is washed through and it can travel easily without sacrificing space. The ASR Outdoor gold panning pocket sluice box features three traditional riffle patterns proven to trap gold flake and nuggets: Hungarian, "V" shape and Square riffle gold traps that are built-in to create water turbulence in the sediment channel. They will trap the gold in your pay dirt and separate the small gold flour in a very effective way that helps to increase your gold prospecting recovery efforts. Coarse pieces will be caught easily in the Hungarian riffle traps while other smaller gold pieces may float over and get caught in the V shape before it has a chance to run out of your sluice box. Using a hybrid sluice design like this one gives you the best chance to maximize your gold recovery for various types of different gold. The ASR Outdoor pocket sluice box for gold panning is a great addition to any seasoned prospector's collection of gold mining equipment as well as a great first-time sluice box option for beginners in their gold panning kits as this small unit takes the guess work out of panning and puts results in your hand. This compact and versatile pocket sluice box takes on the design extensively used during the California gold rush, sluice boxes were developed to have different riffle patterns that work together to sift out raw material while trapping gold flake and small gold flour in. Using a sluice box to pan for gold will allow you to easily identify valuable minerals while disposing of unwanted materials like silt, rock, and sand. Scout and explore new locations before hauling all of your larger gold prospecting equipment out to the field. Use this pocket sluice box to run test concentrate and pay dirt from your site. The ASR Outdoor Pocket Sluice box is a must-have gold panning tool for any gold rush prospector!

narrative: Whether you're new to gold panning or are an experienced gold prospector, you know how important it is to be able to successfully and efficiently classify your raw material down to usable paydirt, either for panning or to run through a sluice box to find gold nuggets, gems or other. The collapsible bucket is a lightweight alternative to a traditional 5 gallon bucket, which can be heavy and cumbersome to use, as well as carry to and from your gold claim site. To use the bucket and stand combo, you simply slide the four arms into the base and rotate until they lock in place. Then place the collapsible bucket over the tops of the arms and secure. Next, add your sifter and you're ready to start classifying your raw material. The 1/4 inch classifier screen we've included in this gold panning kit is made from high impact, durable ABS plastic and will stand up to lots of gold prospecting equipment wear and tear. This sifting screens will allow you to classify down material to usable paydirt, removing larger unwanted materials like rocks and gravels, leaving you with just sand, fine gravels and hopefully gold nuggets! And, as mentioned, the sifters are designed to be stacked on top of the collapsible bucket for quick and efficient processing of unclassified paydirt. The collapsible bucket is made from BPA free silicone, features a sturdy carrying handle, bottom finger indentation and water pouring channel; the bucket can also be employed for tons of other uses outside of gold panning kits like fishing, gardening, as an ice bucket, for camping and much more. Lastly, a handy mesh bag is included to make carrying the collapsible bucket and stand components easier, while allowing for tidy and organized storage when you're not utilizing your gold prospecting equipment to ensure none of the pieces are lost (does not fit the 1/4 inch classifier screen). The collapsible bucket with stand is space saving, compact and lightweight in design, and overall a useful and versatile tool to include with your gold prospecting equipment.

Whether you choose to feed your river sluice or work on gold panning techniques, you’ve got both options with this kit! The 50 inch folding gold sluice box features 18 gauge steel and zinc plated riffles built right into the sediment channel, alongside preinstalled woven vinyl miner’s moss, meaning no assembly is required and it’s ready to go right out of the box. Alternatively, the gold pans included are dual riffle with both a shallow and deep sidewall riffle design; they measure 10, 12 and 14 inches, are molded from one piece of high impact ABS plastic, are lightweight, rust resistant and buoyant in water. If you’re lucky enough to spot gold, you can use the included snuffer bottles to extract gold flakes or nuggets and store them in the screw top glass vials for safe keeping. If not, well, we’re sorry to say, it’s back to the bucket. Good luck out there!
